title: MSc Thesis - KV Cache Compression via Low-Rank Tensor Decomposition
outcome: 2x KV-cache compression at ~1.3% error on K, about 35-40x lower V error than xKV
summary: MSc thesis compressing the runtime KV cache of LLMs as a 3rd-order tensor (Tucker, CP, TT, t-SVD), with per-layer rank allocation framed as constrained optimisation.

star.situation: At scale (e.g. LLaMA-70B at 128K context) the KV cache exceeds 40 GB of GPU memory and becomes the main deployment bottleneck. Prior work (PALU, xKV) compresses weight matrices offline in 2D per-head form, ignoring correlations across heads and tokens in the runtime cache.
star.task: Compress the actual KV cache activations at inference time by treating each layer's cache as a genuine 3rd-order tensor, allocating the compression budget per layer under a global memory constraint.
star.action: Applied Tucker, CP, TT and t-SVD decompositions to runtime cache activations on Mistral-7B-v0.3 (GQA) and LLaMA-2-13B (MHA). The production method is a partial Tucker decomposition plus a JL-rotated 4-bit residual code driven by a joint Lagrangian rank allocator; fused reconstruction-attention kernels to cut decode latency are the next milestone.
star.result: 2x compression holds ~1.3% relative Frobenius error on K and 0.6-1.5% on V, about 35-40x lower V-cache error than xKV at the same ratio. Perplexity, GSM8K 8-shot CoT and RULER retrieval stay within baseline across the 2x-3x free zone.

title: RAG Resume API
outcome: Powers the live AI Q&A assistant embedded in this portfolio site
summary: A Retrieval-Augmented Generation API over my resume and documents: LlamaIndex + Gemini 2.5 Flash, FastAPI on Cloud Run, with rate limiting and a daily budget cap.

star.situation: A static portfolio site offered no interactive way for visitors to query details about my background, projects or experience beyond reading the pages.
star.task: Build and deploy a production-safe RAG backend that indexes personal documents and answers natural-language questions in real time.
star.action: Built a LlamaIndex VectorStoreIndex using gemini-embedding-001 for embeddings and gemini-2.5-flash for generation, served via FastAPI, containerised with Docker, and deployed on Google Cloud Run. Added per-IP rate limiting, a daily Gemini API budget cap, an in-memory query cache, and Google Sheets request logging.
star.result: The assistant is live on the portfolio site, giving visitors interactive Q&A over my documents with production safeguards and scale-to-zero free-tier hosting.

title: Neuro-Symbolic Narrative Similarity (HeteroGNN + Text Fusion)
outcome: 72.5% pairwise narrative-similarity accuracy with an interpretable additive fusion
summary: Fuses a HeteroGNN over LLM-extracted narrative components with Gemini text embeddings to score narrative similarity, with an interpretable additive decomposition.

star.situation: Narrative similarity methods relied on fine-tuned transformer embeddings that gave opaque dense vectors and lacked structural interpretability over extracted story components.
star.task: Build a neuro-symbolic architecture that combines a graph channel over LLM-extracted narrative components with a neural text channel and produce an interpretable fused similarity score.
star.action: Used GPT-OSS 20B to extract narrative components into a schema-validated JSON, built a heterogeneous graph with four node types, trained a HeteroGNN with SAGEConv and triplet loss, then fused graph and Gemini text embeddings via normalised averaging with CORAL domain adaptation.
star.result: Achieved 72.5% accuracy on the pairwise narrative-similarity task. Principal-angle analysis confirmed the two channels occupy near-orthogonal subspaces (85.4 degrees), enabling an exact additive decomposition of the fused cosine score at R-squared = 1.0.

title: Amazon Electronics Recommender System
outcome: NDCG@10 0.305 -> 0.334 (+9.5%) with a Two-Tower MLP, sub-100ms CPU serving
summary: End-to-end recommender on 390K Amazon Electronics interactions: Two-Tower MLP, FAISS retrieval + MLP re-rank, FastAPI on Azure with sub-100ms CPU serving.

star.situation: Building a scalable recommender on the Amazon Electronics dataset (41K users, 137K items, ~390K interactions) meant combining implicit feedback with product metadata and text while keeping inference cheap enough for CPU-only cloud deployment.
star.task: Design, train and deploy a production-ready neural recommender that beats a collaborative-filtering baseline and serves results in under 100ms.
star.action: Implemented and compared collaborative filtering, hybrid SBERT + metadata models, and a Two-Tower MLP; fused SBERT title embeddings into the item tower; built a two-stage FAISS retrieval + MLP re-rank pipeline; bundled inference assets into a single checkpoint to cut cold start from 30-45s to 2-3s; deployed on Azure Container Apps via FastAPI.
star.result: The Two-Tower MLP improved NDCG@10 from 0.305 to 0.334 (about 9.5% relative, validated with paired t-tests over 5 seeds) and lifted cold-start hit rate by ~50%, with sub-100ms warm inference on CPU. Live demo may cold-start from scale-to-zero (first request up to ~30s).

title: Reflective CoT Faithfulness Study (Think Twice)
outcome: RCoT cut bias susceptibility by ~7 points over standard CoT across 650 BBH cases
summary: A reproducible framework testing whether reflective chain-of-thought makes LLM reasoning more faithful, across 13 BIG-Bench Hard categories and two models.

star.situation: Prior work showed CoT rationales can be swayed by irrelevant prompt cues, but whether structured self-reflection (RCoT) reduces this systematic unfaithfulness was untested.
star.task: Design and run a controlled evaluation comparing direct, CoT and reflective-CoT prompting under three bias-injection conditions across two models.
star.action: Co-built a Python evaluation framework running Gemini 2.5 Flash Lite and LLaMA 3.2 3B over 13 BIG-Bench Hard categories (650 instances), injecting three irrelevant biasing cues in zero-shot and few-shot settings with structured JSON output enforcement.
star.result: RCoT reduced bias susceptibility by roughly 7 percentage points over standard CoT, but models still produced plausible fabricated rationales hiding the true cause of biased predictions, showing structured reflection alone is not enough for faithful reasoning.

title: Risk-Averse Optimization (CVaR across 3 Domains)
outcome: CVaR MPC cut constraint violations from 100% to 0% under severe model mismatch
summary: CVaR-based risk-averse optimisation across three domains: nonlinear programming, an SVM classifier, and a 21-day wine-fermentation MPC controller.

star.situation: Standard nominal optimisation ignores tail risk, giving fragile solutions when real-world parameters deviate from assumptions, a problem that recurs in static nonlinear programs, ML classifiers and dynamic control alike.
star.task: Demonstrate CVaR-based risk-averse optimisation as a practical alternative to nominal methods across three structurally different problem classes under parametric uncertainty.
star.action: Formulated CVaR objectives via the Rockafellar-Uryasev form with sample-average approximation; implemented a Wobbly Rosenbrock case in CasADi/IPOPT, an SVM on a cats-vs-dogs dataset in CVXPY/OSQP, and a wine-fermentation nonlinear ODE as a scenario-based receding-horizon MPC over a 21-day horizon.
star.result: Under severe temperature mismatch the CVaR controller cut constraint violations from 100% to 0%; the CVaR-SVM improved mean accuracy from 54.33% to 56.33% and tightened the tail versus the nominal SVM under both Gaussian and heavy-tailed perturbations.

title: ML Kaggle Competitions (Top-3 in Three Challenges)
outcome: Top-3 finish in all three university Kaggle challenges
summary: Top-3 in three university Kaggle challenges spanning classical ML (HistGBM), a from-scratch PyTorch CNN, and few-shot LLM classification with Gemma-3-12B.

star.situation: Three course-integrated Kaggle competitions required competitive ML solutions under hard tooling constraints, each covering a distinct paradigm: tabular ML, image classification and LLM-based inference.
star.task: Build end-to-end, reproducible single-file solutions for tabular classification, image classification with a from-scratch CNN, and LLM-based row-wise classification.
star.action: Engineered temporal and cost features for a HistGradientBoostingClassifier in challenge 1; designed a VGG-style CNN with AdamW, cosine LR scheduling and augmentation (no pretrained weights) in challenge 2; and used few-shot retrieval prompting with 4-bit quantised Gemma-3-12B in challenge 3.
star.result: Top-3 finish in all three competitions, demonstrating breadth across classical ML, deep learning and practical LLM inference within strict academic submission rules.

title: Transformer-Based Sentiment Analysis (YouTube Comments)
outcome: F1 = 0.81 on 10,000+ imbalanced YouTube comments, CI/CD to Docker Hub
summary: Fine-tuned BERT for YouTube comment sentiment, served via a Flask API, containerised with Docker, and shipped through GitHub Actions to Docker Hub.

star.situation: YouTube comment datasets are large, noisy and class-imbalanced, making accurate sentiment classification a practical NLP challenge without a readily deployable solution.
star.task: Fine-tune a BERT model for sentiment classification and deploy it as a production-ready API with automated build and publish workflows.
star.action: Fine-tuned BERT on 10,000+ YouTube comments with PyTorch and Hugging Face, served the model via a Flask REST API in a Docker container, and automated image builds and pushes to Docker Hub with GitHub Actions. An interactive Gradio demo was deployed on Hugging Face Spaces.
star.result: Achieved F1 = 0.81 on the imbalanced dataset, with a CI/CD pipeline that turns every code change into an updated, published Docker image for repeatable deployment.

title: Glaucoma Detection from Retinal Fundus Images
outcome: Published at ICCSP 2020 (IEEE): region-growing segmentation + SVM screening
summary: Automated glaucoma detection from retinal fundus images using optic-disc/cup segmentation and SVM classification. Undergraduate work, published at ICCSP 2020.

star.situation: Glaucoma is a leading cause of blindness, and manual screening from retinal fundus images is time-consuming and requires specialist expertise.
star.task: Design and publish an automated pipeline to detect glaucoma from fundus images using classical computer vision and machine learning.
star.action: Led a 4-member team to build a region-growing segmentation pipeline for optic-disc and cup extraction, followed by SVM classification, implemented in MATLAB.
star.result: The work was accepted and presented at the International Conference on Communication and Signal Processing (ICCSP) 2020 and published on IEEE Xplore, the team's first peer-reviewed publication.
